title: Reindex merges components per id: layout facts from the scan, enrichment from the store, byte-identical when unchanged
context: indexRepo persisted components via replaceAll from fresh drafts: every `hunch index` rewrote created_at/updated_at (permanent git noise in the committed .hunch/) and silently reset enrichment that is not derivable from directory layout — curated responsibility/owners, status, fragility raised by raiseFragility, provenance upgraded past 'inferred'.
decision: indexRepo merges each derived draft with the stored record by id before replaceAll: paths/kind/name come from the scan; responsibility (if set), owners (if set), status, max(fragility), and non-inferred provenance are preserved; created_at always survives; updated_at bumps only when the merged record differs from the stored one (timestamp-blind JSON compare) — an unchanged component is byte-identical across reindexes.
consequences: hunch index is idempotent on committed component JSON — no more timestamp-only diffs
raiseFragility and human curation survive reindex instead of resetting to draft defaults
a component whose directory disappears still drops out (replaceAll semantics unchanged)
alternatives_rejected: Keep replaceAll clobber and gitignore component timestamps: can't ignore fields inside committed JSON, and the enrichment reset was the deeper bug
Preserve the entire previous record when the id matches: layout facts (paths, kind, name) must track the scan or renamed/moved dirs go stale
min instead of max for fragility: reindex would undo synthesis raises
